title: De ce e Simion sclavul lui Trump când alți suveraniști europeni nu sunt?
summary: The article examines why Romanian sovereignist George Simion displays servile behavior toward Trump, unlike European counterparts such as Meloni, Farage, and Le Pen who pushed back against Trump's Greenland threats. Author Cristian Ghinea argues this pattern of external subservience is a recurring theme in Romanian political history, from Antonescu's relationship with Hitler to modern politicians competing to serve foreign powers.
talking_points: European sovereignists like Meloni, Farage, Le Pen, and AfD all rejected Trump's Greenland ambitions, while Simion embarrassingly pandered to the White House during the crisis
Romania's strongest moments of sovereignty historically came through alignment with the European concert of nations, not through isolation or submission to a single power
Romanian sovereignists falsely claim EU membership is colonialism, ignoring that Romania has commissioners, MEPs, veto rights, and a seat at the decision table

There is a recurring Romanian political pattern of competing for subservience to foreign powers, from Antonescu and the Legionnaires competing to serve Hitler, to modern politicians competing to serve Trump
Simion copies MAGA aesthetics as a cargo cult — imitating the form (hats, slogans) without understanding the substance that Western sovereignists possess
author: Cristian Ghinea
